questions.question: How can you create a new column for your text on a page?

options.text: Click the Table icon in the left margin
options.explanation: Clicking the Table icon creates a simple table, not a column layout.

options.text: Type '# ' followed by space to create a header column
options.explanation: Using '# ' creates an H1 heading, not a side-by-side column.

options.text: Click and drag the text you want to move, following the blue guides
options.explanation: You can create columns by dragging text blocks and dropping them using the blue guides that appear.

options.text: Select the 'Turn into database' option from the top menu
options.explanation: This converts a table to a database, which organizes data differently than a column layout.

questions.question: What happens to content in the right-hand column if you view your Notion page on a mobile device?

options.text: The content disappears entirely
options.explanation: The content does not disappear; it is simply repositioned.

options.text: The content in the right column is moved under the content in the left column
options.explanation: On mobile, columns are not supported, so the right column content stacks vertically below the left column content.

options.text: It turns into a database view
options.explanation: Viewing on mobile does not automatically convert a column layout into a database.

options.text: It is deleted automatically
options.explanation: Columns are not deleted when viewed on mobile; they are just reflowed.

questions.question: How do you turn a standard heading (like H2) into a collapsible toggle heading?

options.text: Hover over the heading and select the six-dot icon to create a header row
options.explanation: The six-dot icon is used for making table headers, not for toggling headings.

options.text: Type '/toggle' in the block
options.explanation: There is no '/toggle' command; you must convert the existing heading block.

options.text: Hover over the heading or text and select Turn into, then select Toggle heading
options.explanation: You can convert any heading or text block into a toggle heading by selecting 'Turn into' from the menu.

options.text: Click the Cmd/Ctrl + / command and select 'Collapse'
options.explanation: The Cmd/Ctrl + / command converts text to a heading, not a toggle.

questions.question: Which of the following is a correct method to add a divider to a page?

options.text: Type '---' (three hyphens) followed by space
options.explanation: Three hyphens in a row will automatically create a divider block.

options.text: Type '/divider' and press Enter
options.explanation: The correct slash command is '/div', not '/divider'.

options.text: Hover over the right margin and click the plus icon
options.explanation: The plus icon appears in the left margin, not the right.

options.text: Drag the bottom-right corner of a table block
options.explanation: Dragging the bottom-right corner of a table is used to resize the table itself, not create a divider.

questions.question: How can you add a row to an existing simple table?

options.text: Hover over the left side of the table and click +
options.explanation: Clicking the left side adds a column, not a row.

options.text: Hover over the bottom of the table and click +
options.explanation: Hovering over the bottom of the table reveals a + button to add a new row.

options.text: Drag the bottom-right corner to the right
options.explanation: Dragging the bottom-right corner adjusts the width and can add rows/columns, but it is not the specific method described for adding just a row.

options.text: Click the six-dot icon on the first cell
options.explanation: The six-dot icon is used to make a column a header, not to add a row.

questions.question: You want to change the color of your Table of Contents block. What happens if you change the color?

options.text: It only applies to the text of the headings in the TOC
options.explanation: The color applies to the entire Table of Contents block, not just individual text items.

options.text: It applies to the whole Table of Contents
options.explanation: When you change the color of a Table of Contents, it applies to the entire list of headings.

options.text: It changes the color of the individual headings on the page
options.explanation: Changing the TOC color does not alter the color of the headings on the main page.

options.text: It is not possible to color a Table of Contents block
options.explanation: You can select the Color option from the menu to change the TOC's appearance.

questions.question: What is the Markdown shortcut for creating an H2 heading?

options.explanation: The '#' symbol creates an H1 heading.
Two hash symbols (##) followed by a space creates an H2 heading.
The '###' symbol creates an H3 heading.
Triple hashes create an H3, not an H2.

questions.question: Can you paste multiple cells at once into a simple table?

options.text: Yes, you can select multiple cells and paste them at once
options.explanation: At this time, you cannot paste multiple simple table cells at once.

options.text: No, you must add cells one by one
options.explanation: The help center explicitly notes that pasting multiple cells at once is not currently supported for simple tables.

options.text: Yes, but only if the table is empty
options.explanation: This limitation applies regardless of whether the table is empty or has existing content.

options.text: No, simple tables do not support pasting at all
options.explanation: You can paste text into a cell, just not multiple cells simultaneously.

questions.question: How do you remove an empty column from a layout?

options.text: Hover over the column and click the trash icon
options.explanation: There is no trash icon for columns; you must use the drag and drop method or the Delete option.

options.text: Drag the content back under the left column until the blue guide spans the width of the page
options.explanation: This is the method for removing columns when they have content. If the column is empty, this won't work.

options.text: Click the '⋮⋮' icon and select Delete
options.explanation: When a column is empty, clicking the handle (⋮⋮) and selecting Delete removes the column format.

options.text: Turn the column into a database
options.explanation: Converting a column to a database isn't the standard way to remove an empty column; Delete is the specific action.

questions.question: When viewing a page-level Table of Contents, which headings are indented?

options.text: H1 headings are indented to show they are the main topic
options.explanation: Only H2 and H3 headings are included and indented to reflect the page structure.

options.text: H2 and H3 headings are indented to reflect the page structure
options.explanation: The Table of Contents lists all headings; H2s and H3s appear indented to show their hierarchy relative to H1s.

options.text: All headings are indented the same amount
options.explanation: Indentation is used to visually distinguish between secondary (H2) and tertiary (H3) headings.

options.text: Only headings that have been converted to toggles are indented
options.explanation: Toggle headings work just like regular headings regarding the Table of Contents listing.
